exercises.title: Security Category Mapping
exercises.description: Map NIST security categories to real-world applications and determine appropriate security levels.
tasks: Define each of the 5 NIST security categories
Justify your categorizations based on data sensitivity and lifetime
exercises.expectedOutput: A comprehensive mapping document with justifications for each application's security category
hints: Consider how long the data needs to remain confidential
Government classified data typically requires Category 5
Consumer applications might be fine with Category 1

exercises.title: Performance Trade-off Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze the performance trade-offs between the selected NIST standards.
tasks: Create a comparison table of ML-KEM, ML-DSA, and SLH-DSA
Include key sizes, ciphertext/signature sizes, and operation speeds
Identify scenarios where each algorithm is the best choice
Explain why having multiple standards is valuable
exercises.expectedOutput: A detailed comparison matrix with scenario recommendations
hints: SLH-DSA has small keys but large signatures
ML-DSA offers the best balance for general use
Consider both bandwidth-constrained and CPU-constrained scenarios

exercises.title: Cryptanalysis Impact Assessment
exercises.description: Research how cryptanalysis results affected the NIST PQC competition.
tasks: Identify at least 5 significant cryptanalytic results during the competition
Explain the impact of each on the affected algorithm
Discuss how surviving algorithms were strengthened
Analyze what the Rainbow and SIKE breaks teach us
exercises.expectedOutput: A research document detailing cryptanalysis impact with at least 5 examples
hints: Look at IACR ePrint archive for cryptanalysis papers
Parameter changes between rounds often reflect cryptanalysis
The SIKE break is particularly instructive

exercises.title: Evaluation Criteria Design
exercises.description: Design evaluation criteria for a hypothetical future cryptographic competition.
tasks: Identify any criteria you would add or modify
Explain how your criteria would handle the security/performance trade-off
Describe how you would ensure mathematical diversity
exercises.expectedOutput: A proposal document for evaluation criteria with justifications
hints: Consider lessons learned from SIKE and Rainbow breaks
